# 🌈 Colorist for Python 🌈
Lightweight Python package that makes it easy and fast to print colored text in the terminal.

Ready to try? See [how to install](https://jakob-bagterp.github.io/colorist-for-python/getting-started/installation/).

## Getting Started
### Print Line of Colored Text
How to print a full line of colored text in the terminal:

```python
from colorist import green, yellow, red

green("This is GREEN!")
yellow("This is YELLOW!")
red("This is RED!")
```

How it appears in the terminal:

![Example of full line of green, yellow, red colored text printed in a terminal window](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_full_text_green_yellow_red.png)

### Print Mixed Text Colors
How to customize colors inside a paragraph and print it in the terminal:

```python
from colorist import Color

print(f"I want {Color.RED}red{Color.OFF} color inside this paragraph")

print(f"Both {Color.GREEN}green{Color.OFF} and {Color.YELLOW}yellow{Color.OFF} are nice colors")
```

How it appears in the terminal:

![Example of white text mixed with green, yellow, red colors printed in a terminal window](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_custom_text_green_yellow_red.png)

## Other Styling Options
### Print Bright Colors
Most terminals support bright colors that stand more out:

```python
from colorist import BrightColor

print(f"I want {BrightColor.CYAN}cyan{BrightColor.OFF} color inside this paragraph")
```

How it appears in the terminal:

![Example of white text mixed with cyan printed in a terminal window](/docs/assets/images/examples/bright_color_custom_text_cyan.png)

Remember to use `Color.OFF` or `BrightColor.OFF` every time you want to revert back to the default terminal text style. Otherwise, the color may spill over and into other terminal messages.

### Print Background Colors
```python
from colorist import bg_green, bg_yellow, bg_red

bg_green("This is GREEN background!")
bg_yellow("This is YELLOW background!")
bg_red("This is RED background!")
```

How it appears in the terminal:

![Example of white text and colored backgrounds if green, yellow, red printed in a terminal window](/docs/assets/images/examples/bg_color_full_text_green_yellow_red.png)

Background colors can also be mixed inside a paragraph:

```python
from colorist import BgColor

print(f"I want {BgColor.RED}red{BgColor.OFF} background color inside this paragraph")

print(f"Both {BgColor.GREEN}green{BgColor.OFF} and {BgColor.YELLOW}yellow{BgColor.OFF} are nice background colors")
```

How it appears in the terminal:

![Example of black, green, yellow, red background colors mixed inside paragraph printed in a terminal window](/docs/assets/images/examples/bg_color_custom_text_green_yellow_red.png)

```python
from colorist import BgBrightColor

print(f"I want {BgBrightColor.CYAN}cyan{BgBrightColor.OFF} background color inside this paragraph")
```

How it appears in the terminal:

![Example of white text on a cyan background color printed in a terminal window](/docs/assets/images/examples/bg_bright_color_custom_text_cyan.png)

As with text colors, remember to use `BgColor.OFF` or `BgBrightColor.OFF` every time you want to revert back to the default terminal text style. Otherwise, the color may spill over and into other terminal messages.

## Foreground Text
| Color | Full Text Function | Custom | Example |
| ----- | ------------------ | ------ | ------- |
| ![Green](/docs/assets/images/colors/green_16x16.png) | `green("text")` | `Color.GREEN` | ![Green text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/green_full_text_167x16.png) |
| ![Yellow](/docs/assets/images/colors/yellow_16x16.png) | `yellow("text")` | `Color.YELLOW` | ![Yellow text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/yellow_full_text_167x16.png) |
| ![Red](/docs/assets/images/colors/red_16x16.png) | `red("text")` | `Color.RED` | ![Red text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/red_full_text_167x16.png) |
| ![Magenta](/docs/assets/images/colors/magenta_16x16.png) | `magenta("text")` | `Color.MAGENTA` | ![Magenta text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/magenta_full_text_167x16.png) |
| ![Blue](/docs/assets/images/colors/blue_16x16.png) | `blue("text")` | `Color.BLUE` | ![Blue text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/blue_full_text_167x16.png) |
| ![Cyan](/docs/assets/images/colors/cyan_16x16.png) | `cyan("text")` | `Color.CYAN` | ![Cyan text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/cyan_full_text_167x16.png) |
| ![White](/docs/assets/images/colors/white_16x16.png) | `white("text")` | `Color.WHITE` | ![White text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/white_full_text_167x16.png) |
| ![Black](/docs/assets/images/colors/black_16x16.png) | `black("text")` | `Color.BLACK` | ![Black text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/black_full_text_167x16.png) |
| - | - | `Color.DEFAULT` | - |
| - | - | `Color.OFF` | - |
| ![Bright green](/docs/assets/images/colors/bright_green_16x16.png) | `bright_green("text")` | `BrightColor.GREEN` | ![Bright green text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/bright_green_full_text_167x16.png) |
| ![Bright yellow](/docs/assets/images/colors/bright_yellow_16x16.png) | `bright_yellow("text")` | `BrightColor.YELLOW` | ![Bright yellow text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/bright_yellow_full_text_167x16.png) |
| ![Bright red](/docs/assets/images/colors/bright_red_16x16.png) | `bright_red("text")` | `BrightColor.RED` | ![Bright red text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/bright_red_full_text_167x16.png) |
| ![Bright magenta](/docs/assets/images/colors/bright_magenta_16x16.png) | `bright_magenta("text")` | `BrightColor.MAGENTA` | ![Bright magenta text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/bright_magenta_full_text_167x16.png) |
| ![Bright blue](/docs/assets/images/colors/bright_blue_16x16.png) | `bright_blue("text")` | `BrightColor.BLUE` | ![Bright blue text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/bright_blue_full_text_167x16.png) |
| ![Bright cyan](/docs/assets/images/colors/bright_cyan_16x16.png) | `bright_cyan("text")` | `BrightColor.CYAN` | ![Bright cyan text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/bright_cyan_full_text_167x16.png) |
| ![Bright white](/docs/assets/images/colors/bright_white_16x16.png) | `bright_white("text")` | `BrightColor.WHITE` | ![Bright white text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/bright_white_full_text_167x16.png) |
| ![Bright black](/docs/assets/images/colors/bright_black_16x16.png) | `bright_black("text")` | `BrightColor.BLACK` | ![Bright black text color in terminal](/docs/assets/images/examples/color_map/bright_black_full_text_167x16.png) |

modules.name: colorist.print.background.rgb
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

functions.name: colorist.print.background.rgb.bg_rgb
documentation: Prints full line of text on custom RGB-colored background. Values for red, green, blue can be between `0` and `255`.

methods.name: colorist.print.background.rgb.MkDocstringsWrapper.bg_rgb
documentation:
Prints full line of text on custom RGB-colored background. Args: text (str): Text to be printed on colored background. red (int): Number between `0` and `255`. green (int): Number between `0` and `255`. blue (int): Number between `0` and `255`. Example: ```python linenums="1" from colorist import bg_rgb bg_rgb("I want this background in blue RGB colors", 0, 128, 255) ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% I want this background in blue RGB colors

functions.name: colorist.print.background.hex.bg_hex
documentation: Prints full line of text on colored background defined in Hex color. Expects valid Hex color value with or without hashtag, for instance `#B4FBB8` or `B4FBB8`, `#B4F` or `B4F`.

methods.name: colorist.print.background.hex.MkDocstringsWrapper.bg_hex
documentation:
Prints full line of text on colored background defined in Hex color. Args: text (str): Text to be printed on colored background. hex (str): Hex color value with or without hashtag, for instance `#B4FBB8` or `B4FBB8`, `#B4F` or `B4F`. Example: ```python linenums="1" from colorist import bg_hex bg_hex("I want this background in coral Hex colors", "#ff7f50") ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% I want this background in coral Hex colors

modules.name: colorist.print.background.hsl
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

functions.name: colorist.print.background.hsl.bg_hsl
documentation: Prints full line of text on custom HSL-colored background. Value for hue can be number between `0` and `360` degrees, while saturation and lightness can be a percentage between `0` and `100` %.

methods.name: colorist.print.background.hsl.MkDocstringsWrapper.bg_hsl
documentation:
Prints full line of text on custom HSL-colored background. Args: text (str): Text to be printed on colored background. hue (float): Number between `0` and `360` degrees. saturation (float): Percentage between `0` and `100` %. lightness (float): Percentage between `0` and `100` %. Example: ```python linenums="1" from colorist import bg_hsl bg_hsl("I want this background in green HSL colors", 120, 50, 50) ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% I want this background in green HSL colors

methods.name: colorist.print.background.vga.MkDocstringsWrapper.bg_vga
documentation:

Example: ```python linenums="1" from colorist import vga bg_vga("I want this background in blue VGA colors", 33) ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% I want this background in blue VGA colors

modules.name: colorist.print.foreground.rgb
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

functions.name: colorist.print.foreground.rgb.rgb
documentation: Prints full line of custom RGB-colored text. Values for red, green, blue can be between `0` and `255`.

methods.name: colorist.print.foreground.rgb.MkDocstringsWrapper.rgb
documentation:
Prints full line of custom RGB-colored text. Args: text (str): Text to be printed with color. red (int): Number between `0` and `255`. green (int): Number between `0` and `255`. blue (int): Number between `0` and `255`. Example: ```python linenums="1" from colorist import rgb rgb("I want this text in blue RGB colors", 0, 128, 255) ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% I want this text in blue RGB colors

methods.name: colorist.print.foreground.color.MkDocstringsWrapper.black
documentation:
Prints full line of black text. Args: text (str): Text to be printed with color. Example: ```python title="" linenums="1" from colorist import black black("This is BLACK!") ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% This is BLACK!

documentation:
Prints full line of red text. Args: text (str): Text to be printed with color. Example: ```python title="" linenums="1" from colorist import red red("This is RED!") ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% This is RED!

modules.name: colorist.print.foreground.hsl
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

functions.name: colorist.print.foreground.hsl.hsl
documentation: Prints full line of custom HSL-colored text. Value for hue can be number between `0` and `360` degrees, while saturation and lightness can be a percentage between `0` and `100` %.

methods.name: colorist.print.foreground.hsl.MkDocstringsWrapper.hsl
documentation:
Prints full line of custom HSL-colored text. Args: text (str): Text to be printed on colored background. hue (float): Number between `0` and `360` degrees. saturation (float): Percentage between `0` and `100` %. lightness (float): Percentage between `0` and `100` %. Example: ```python linenums="1" from colorist import hsl hsl("I want this text in green HSL colors", 120, 50, 50) ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% I want this text in green HSL colors

modules.name: colorist.print.foreground.hex
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

functions.name: colorist.print.foreground.hex.hex
documentation: Prints full line of text in Hex color. Expects valid Hex color value with or without hashtag, for instance `#B4FBB8` or `B4FBB8`, `#B4F` or `B4F`.

methods.name: colorist.print.foreground.hex.MkDocstringsWrapper.hex
documentation:
Prints full line of text in Hex color. Args: text (str): Text to be printed with color. hex (str): Hex color value with or without hashtag, for instance `#B4FBB8` or `B4FBB8`, `#B4F` or `B4F`. Example: ```python linenums="1" from colorist import hex hex("I want this text in coral Hex colors", "#ff7f50") ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% I want this text in coral Hex colors

Abstract base class used to documentation standalone functions now that MkDocstrings (as far as I know) only support reference of functions within classes.

Should not used for other purposes than documentation.

modules.name: colorist.model.background.color
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

classes.name: colorist.model.background.color.BgColor
documentation:
Options for standard background colors. Implements ANSI escape codes for printing color, effects, and styling in the terminal.

Reference: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code

modules.name: colorist.model.background.rgb
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

classes.name: colorist.model.background.rgb.BgColorRGB

methods.name: colorist.model.background.rgb.BgColorRGB.__init__
documentation:
Args: red (int): Number between `0` and `255`. green (int): Number between `0` and `255`. blue (int): Number between `0` and `255`. Example: ```python linenums="1" from colorist import BgColorRGB bg_steel_blue = BgColorRGB(70, 130, 180) print(f"I want to use {bg_steel_blue}steel blue{bg_steel_blue.OFF} background color inside this paragraph") ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% I want to use steel blue background color inside this paragraph
Class for custom 16-bit RGB background color instances.

modules.name: colorist.model.background.hex
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

classes.name: colorist.model.background.hex.BgColorHex

methods.name: colorist.model.background.hex.BgColorHex.__init__
documentation:
Args: hex (str): Hex color value with or without hashtag, for instance `#B4FBB8` or `B4FBB8`, `#B4F` or `B4F`. Example: ```python linenums="1" from colorist import BgColorHex bg_mint_green = BgColorHex("#99ff99") print(f"I want to use {bg_mint_green}mint green{bg_mint_green.OFF} background color inside this paragraph") ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% I want to use mint green background color inside this paragraph
Class for custom background defined in Hex color.

modules.name: colorist.model.background.bright_color
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

classes.name: colorist.model.background.bright_color.BgBrightColor
documentation:
Options for bright background colors (not standard, but mostly supported). Implements ANSI escape codes for printing color, effects, and styling in the terminal.

Reference: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code

modules.name: colorist.model.background.hsl
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

classes.name: colorist.model.background.hsl.BgColorHSL

methods.name: colorist.model.background.hsl.BgColorHSL.__init__
documentation:
Args: hue (float): Number between `0` and `360` degrees. saturation (float): Percentage between `0` and `100` %. lightness (float): Percentage between `0` and `100` %. Example: ```python linenums="1" from colorist import BgColorHSL bg_steel_gray = BgColorHSL(190, 2, 49) print(f"I want to use {bg_steel_gray}steel gray{bg_steel_gray.OFF} background color inside this paragraph") ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% I want to use steel gray background color inside this paragraph
Class for custom HSL background color.

methods.name: colorist.print.effect.MkDocstringsWrapper.effect_bold

type.name: Color | BrightColor | ColorVGA | ColorRGB | ColorHSL | ColorHex | BgColor | BgBrightColor | BgColorVGA | BgColorRGB | BgColorHSL | BgColorHex | str | None

documentation:
Prints full line of text with bold styling. Args: text (str): Text to be printed with bold styling. color (Color | BrightColor | ColorVGA | ColorRGB | ColorHSL | ColorHex | BgColor | BgBrightColor | BgColorVGA | BgColorRGB | BgColorHSL | BgColorHex | str | None, optional): Optionally add color to text. Example: ```python title="" linenums="1" from colorist import effect_bold, Color, BgColor effect_bold("This is BOLD") effect_bold("This is BOLD", Color.BLUE) effect_bold("This is BOLD", BgColor.RED) effect_bold("This is BOLD", BgColorHSL(190, 2, 49)) ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% This is BOLD
    % This is BOLD
    % This is BOLD
    % This is BOLD

methods.name: colorist.print.effect.MkDocstringsWrapper.effect_dim

type.name: Color | BrightColor | ColorVGA | ColorRGB | ColorHSL | ColorHex | BgColor | BgBrightColor | BgColorVGA | BgColorRGB | BgColorHSL | BgColorHex | str | None

documentation:
Prints full line of text with dim styling. Args: text (str): Text to be printed with dim effect. color (Color | BrightColor | ColorVGA | ColorRGB | ColorHSL | ColorHex | BgColor | BgBrightColor | BgColorVGA | BgColorRGB | BgColorHSL | BgColorHex | str | None, optional): Optionally add color to text. Example: ```python title="" linenums="1" from colorist import effect_dim, BrightColor, BgColor effect_dim("This is DIMMED") effect_dim("This is DIMMED", BrightColor.GREEN) effect_dim("This is DIMMED", BgColor.MAGENTA) effect_dim("This is DIMMED", ColorHex("#ff5733")) ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% This is DIMMED
    % This is DIMMED
    % This is DIMMED
    % This is DIMMED

methods.name: colorist.print.effect.MkDocstringsWrapper.effect_underline

type.name: Color | BrightColor | ColorVGA | ColorRGB | ColorHSL | ColorHex | BgColor | BgBrightColor | BgColorVGA | BgColorRGB | BgColorHSL | BgColorHex | str | None

documentation:
Prints full line of text with underline styling. Args: text (str): Text to be printed with underline styling. color (Color | BrightColor | ColorVGA | ColorRGB | ColorHSL | ColorHex | BgColor | BgBrightColor | BgColorVGA | BgColorRGB | BgColorHSL | BgColorHex | str | None, optional): Optionally add color to text. Example: ```python title="" linenums="1" from colorist import effect_underline, Color, BgBrightColor effect_underline("This is UNDERLINED") effect_underline("This is UNDERLINED", Color.CYAN) effect_underline("This is UNDERLINED", BgBrightColor.BLUE) effect_underline("This is UNDERLINED", ColorHSL(60, 56, 43)) ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% This is UNDERLINED
    % This is UNDERLINED
    % This is UNDERLINED
    % This is UNDERLINED

methods.name: colorist.print.effect.MkDocstringsWrapper.effect_blink

type.name: Color | BrightColor | ColorVGA | ColorRGB | ColorHSL | ColorHex | BgColor | BgBrightColor | BgColorVGA | BgColorRGB | BgColorHSL | BgColorHex | str | None

documentation:
Prints full line of text with blink effect. Args: text (str): Text to be printed with blink effect. color (Color | BrightColor | ColorVGA | ColorRGB | ColorHSL | ColorHex | BgColor | BgBrightColor | BgColorVGA | BgColorRGB | BgColorHSL | BgColorHex | str | None, optional): Optionally add color to text. Example: ```python title="" linenums="1" from colorist import effect_blink, BrightColor, BgColor effect_blink("This is BLINKING") effect_blink("This is BLINKING", BrightColor.YELLOW) effect_blink("This is BLINKING", BgColor.GREEN) effect_blink("This is BLINKING", BgColorRGB(70, 130, 180)) ``` How it appears in the terminal:

    % This is BLINKING
    % This is BLINKING
    % This is BLINKING

methods.name: colorist.print.effect.MkDocstringsWrapper.effect_reverse

type.name: Color | BrightColor | ColorVGA | ColorRGB | ColorHSL | ColorHex | BgColor | BgBrightColor | BgColorVGA | BgColorRGB | BgColorHSL | BgColorHex | str | None

documentation:
Prints full line of text with reversed foreground and background color effect. Args: text (str): Text to be printed with reverse effect. color (Color | BrightColor | ColorVGA | ColorRGB | ColorHSL | ColorHex | BgColor | BgBrightColor | BgColorVGA | BgColorRGB | BgColorHSL | BgColorHex | str | None, optional): Optionally add color to text. Example: ```python title="" linenums="1" from colorist import effect_reverse, Color, BgColor effect_reverse("This is REVERSED") effect_reverse("This is REVERSED", Color.CYAN) effect_reverse("This is REVERSED", BgColor.RED) effect_reverse("This is REVERSED", ColorRGB(194, 145, 164)) ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% This is REVERSED

    % This is REVERSED
    % This is REVERSED

Example: ```python title="" linenums="1" from colorist import effect_hide, Color, BgColor effect_hide("This is HIDDEN") effect_hide("This is HIDDEN", Color.BLUE) effect_hide("This is HIDDEN", BgColor.RED) effect_hide("This is HIDDEN", BgColorHex("#99ff99")) ``` How it appears in the terminal:

Class for custom 16-bit RGB foreground text color.

modules.name: colorist.model.foreground.color
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

classes.name: colorist.model.foreground.color.Color
documentation:
Options for standard text colors. Implements ANSI escape codes for printing color, effects, and styling in the terminal.

Reference: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code

modules.name: colorist.model.foreground.bright_color
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

classes.name: colorist.model.foreground.bright_color.BrightColor
documentation:
Options for bright text colors (not standard, but mostly supported). Implements ANSI escape codes for printing color, effects, and styling in the terminal.

Reference: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code

modules.name: colorist.model.foreground.hsl
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

classes.name: colorist.model.foreground.hsl.ColorHSL

methods.name: colorist.model.foreground.hsl.ColorHSL.__init__
documentation:
Args: hue (float): Number between `0` and `360` degrees. saturation (float): Percentage between `0` and `100` %. lightness (float): Percentage between `0` and `100` %. Example: ```python linenums="1" from colorist import ColorHSL mustard_green = ColorHSL(60, 56, 43) print(f"I want to use {mustard_green}mustard green{mustard_green.OFF} color inside this paragraph") ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% I want to use mustard green color inside this paragraph
Class for custom HSL foreground text color.

modules.name: colorist.model.foreground.hex
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

classes.name: colorist.model.foreground.hex.ColorHex

methods.name: colorist.model.foreground.hex.ColorHex.__init__
documentation:
Args: hex (str): Hex color value with or without hashtag, for instance `#B4FBB8` or `B4FBB8`, `#B4F` or `B4F`. Example: ```python linenums="1" from colorist import ColorHex watermelon_red = ColorHex("#ff5733") print(f"I want to use {watermelon_red}watermelon red{watermelon_red.OFF} color inside this paragraph") ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% I want to use watermelon red color inside this paragraph
Class for custom foreground text defined in Hex color.

modules.name: colorist.model.foreground.vga
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

classes.name: colorist.model.foreground.vga.ColorVGA

methods.name: colorist.model.foreground.vga.ColorVGA.__init__
documentation:
Args: vga (int): Number between `0` and `255`. Example: ```python linenums="1" from colorist import ColorVGA lime_green = ColorVGA(120) print(f"I want to use {lime_green}lime green{lime_green.OFF} color inside this paragraph") ``` How it appears in the terminal: 
% I want to use lime green color inside this paragraph
Class for custom 8-bit VGA foreground text color.

modules.name: colorist.helper.print
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

functions.name: colorist.helper.print.color
documentation: Helper function to print a full line of colored text (options for standard ANSI colors in both foreground and background) in the terminal.

functions.name: colorist.helper.print.effect
documentation: Helper function to print a full line of text with custom effect in the terminal.

functions.name: colorist.helper.print.normalize_input

type.name: FgColor_ABC | BgColor_ABC | VGA_ABC | RGB_ABC | HSL_ABC | Hex_ABC | Effect_ABC | str | None

documentation: Normalize and convert color classes to string of ANSI escape code for print methods, especially converting None values to empty string.

modules.name: colorist.constants.ansi
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

classes.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiColor

class_variables.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiColor.DEFAULT
documentation: Not standard, but mostly supported.
ANSI color palette 0-7 to be mixed with AnsiColorSelector for foreground (30-37), background (40-47) and other combinations.

classes.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiColorSelector

class_variables.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiColorSelector.FOREGROUND
documentation: Mix with AnsiColor to select foreground 8 colors palette: 30-37.

class_variables.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiColorSelector.BACKGROUND
documentation: Mix with AnsiColor to select background 8 colors palette: 40-47.

class_variables.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiColorSelector.BRIGHT_FOREGROUND
documentation: Mix with AnsiColor to select bright foreground 8 colors palette: 90-97.

class_variables.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiColorSelector.BRIGHT_BACKGROUND
documentation: Mix with AnsiColor to select bright background 8 colors palette: 100-107.
ANSI color selector to be mixed with AnsiColor for foreground (30-37), background (40-47) and other combinations.

classes.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiVgaColorSelector

class_variables.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiVgaColorSelector.FOREGROUND
documentation: Parameter for RGB foreground color sequence: 38;2;r;g;b.

class_variables.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiVgaColorSelector.BACKGROUND
documentation: Parameter for RGB background color sequence: 48;2;r;g;b.
ANSI VGA color selector with 8-bit color palette.

classes.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiRgbColorSelector

class_variables.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiRgbColorSelector.FOREGROUND
documentation: Parameter for RGB foreground color sequence: 38;2;r;g;b.

class_variables.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiRgbColorSelector.BACKGROUND
documentation: Parameter for RGB background color sequence: 48;2;r;g;b.
ANSI 24-bit RGB color selector to be mixed with values for red, green, blue.

classes.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiEffect
documentation: ANSI effect codes to be mixed with effect selector.

classes.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiEffectSelector

class_variables.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiEffectSelector.ON
documentation: Mix with AnsiEffect to select effects: 1-8.

class_variables.name: colorist.constants.ansi.AnsiEffectSelector.OFF
documentation: Mix with AnsiEffect to select effects: 21-28.
ANSI effect selector to be mixed with AnsiEffect for on (1-8) or off (21-28).

modules.name: colorist.helper
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

modules.name: colorist.helper.generate
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

functions.name: colorist.helper.generate.control_sequence_inducer
documentation: Control Sequence Inducer (CSI) marks the beginning of a control sequence, e.g. "\u1b[" in "\u1b[31m".

functions.name: colorist.helper.generate.ansi_standard_color_sequence
documentation: Generate standard ANSI color sequence, e.g. "\u1b[31m".

functions.name: colorist.helper.generate.ansi_vga_color_sequence
documentation: Generate ANSI 8-bit VGA color sequence, e.g. "\u1b[38;5;166m".

functions.name: colorist.helper.generate.ansi_rgb_color_sequence
documentation: Generate ANSI 16-bit RGB color sequence, e.g. "\u1b[38;2;r;g;bm".

modules.name: colorist.helper.error
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

modules.name: colorist.helper.convert
documentation: Copyright 2022 – present, Jakob Bagterp. BSD 3-Clause license and refer to LICENSE file.

functions.name: colorist.helper.convert.normalize_colorsys_rgb_to_real_rgb
documentation: Since Colorsys outputs RGB values as floats between 0.0 an 1.0, we need to normalize this to a standard RGB scale from 0 to 255.

functions.name: colorist.helper.convert.normalize_hue_degrees_to_colorsys_hue
documentation: When converting HLS or HSV with Colorsys, we need to prepare hue between 0 and 360 degrees to a float between 0.0 and 1.0.

functions.name: colorist.helper.convert.normalize_percentage_to_colorsys_value
documentation: When converting HLS or HSV with Colorsys, we need to prepare lightness, saturation, etc. percentages between 0% and 100% to a float between 0.0 and 1.0.

functions.name: colorist.helper.convert.hex_to_rgb
documentation: Convert Hex color to RGB. Expects valid Hex color value with or without hashtag, for instance #B4FBB8 or B4FBB8, #B4F or B4F.
